title: Junior Field Sales Partner - Roma (x/f/m)
description:
**Quale****sarà il tuo ruolo**
------------------------------



Doctolib è alla ricerca di un/una **Junior Field Sales Partner** per unirsi al team e sviluppare le attività commerciali a **Roma**.



Come **Junior Field Sales Partner**, farai parte di un team di professionisti impegnati sul campo a semplificare, attraverso le nostre soluzioni, la vita del personale sanitario e l'accesso alle cure delle persone.



In questo ruolo, sarai il primo punto di contatto tra Doctolib e i **medici del territorio** e seguirai tutto il ciclo di vendita dei servizi offerti da Doctolib.



In particolare ti occuperai di:


**Prospecting:**


* Contattare i medici nella tua zona (con chiamate a freddo, email e visite in presenza) per generare nuove opportunità in modo scalabile
* Qualificare i lead gestendo follow\-up cadenzati per far avanzare la pipeline con continuità.


**Appuntamenti, demo e conversione:**


* Visitare gli studi degli MMG e condurre demo, focalizzandosi sui benefici più rilevanti per la pratica
* Gestire domande e obiezioni guidando il prospect verso una decisione rapida.


**Chiusura e handover:**


* Presentare l'offerta e portare la trattativa fino alla firma, con un ciclo di vendita breve
* Essere il punto di riferimento del medico durante l'installazione e il training iniziale del prodotto
* Assicurare un handover puntuale al team di onboarding per un avvio fluido.


**Pipeline e KPI:**


* Aggiornare il CRM e monitorare KPIs (attività, conversion rate, win rate, tempo medio di chiusura) per migliorare costantemente le performance.


Durante tutti gli step di vendita avrai modo di raccogliere feedback sia dai tuoi colleghi/e che dai clienti. Sarà importante per condividere best practices e suggerimenti per migliorare le attività del team Sales e contribuire al successo di Doctolib in Italia.

  


**Cosa ci aspettiamo da te**
----------------------------



Prima di continuare a leggere, se il profilo descritto non è perfettamente aderente al tuo attuale profilo professionale ma ritieni che questa descrizione corrisponda alle tue capacità e alle tue esigenze, ti invitiamo a proseguire!



Questo ruolo potrebbe fare per te se:


* Hai avuto una prima esperienza di vendita a contatto diretto con il cliente (e.g., retail, hospitality, food \& beverage) e ti piace stare in prima linea
* Hai già lavorato per obiettivi, comprendi i KPI e ti impegni a raggiungerli e superarli, incentivato/a da un bonus competitivo senza cap
* Sei proattivo/a, organizzato/a e ti metti in gioco con entusiasmo
* Hai tanta energia, spirito imprenditoriale e voglia di imparare in fretta
* Sei in possesso di patente di guida e disponibile a spostamenti nella zona assegnata
* Hai ottime doti di comunicazione e relazione interpersonale.


Ora, sarebbe fantastico se:


* Hai esperienza o forte interesse per il mondo della sanità
* Hai un buon livello di inglese.

  


**Cosa offriamo**
-----------------


* Un programma di onboarding dedicato \- la Doctolib Sales Academy!
* Auto aziendale ad uso promiscuo
* Assicurazione sanitaria integrativa, con la possibilità di includere i familiari conviventi
* Food offer: buoni pasto elettronici (8,00€/giorno lavorato)
* Wellbeing e sport: offriamo servizi per la salute mentale in collaborazione con il nostro partner moka.care e partnership sportiva con Wellhub
* Un programma di supporto alla genitorialità che include diverse iniziative, tra cui il congedo parentale retribuito al 100%
* Per chi assiste familiari non autosufficienti e per i lavoratori con disabilità, è previsto un pacchetto che include un adattamento della policy di lavoro da remoto, giorni aggiuntivi di permesso per motivi sanitari e sostegno psicologico
* Partnership con Ciao Elsa per accesso ai fondi pensione
* Vaccinazione antinfluenzale offerta ogni anno in inverno e diverse iniziative di prevenzione durante l'anno
* Adesione al piano di incentivi a lungo termine: DoctoGrowth
* Supporto alla mobilità internazionale e accesso ai nostri tool e programmi di upskilling
* Contributo forfettario mensile per internet e elettricità
* Convenzioni aziendali con diversi partner su tutta Italia: Corporate Benefits, Ennevolte, sconti dedicati e promozioni.

  


**Il processo di selezione**
----------------------------


* Colloquio con il team di Talent Acquisition
* Colloquio con l'hiring manager
* Colloquio finale
* Verifica di una referenza
* Offerta

  


**Descrizione della posizione**
-------------------------------


* Contratto a tempo indeterminato full time
* Area di riferimento: Roma, Lazio
* Data di inizio: Settembre 2026
* Pacchetto totale: €40\.000 \- €48\.000 annui lordi (incluso bonus legato alla performance)
* Bonus senza tetto (uncapped) al superamento degli obiettivi


*In Doctolib, ci impegniamo a migliorare l'accesso all'assistenza sanitaria per tutte le persone. Questo si riflette nel nostro processo di selezione. Valutiamo i profili esclusivamente in base alle loro qualifiche e alla loro motivazione, senza nessuna forma di discriminazione.*  

  

*Più si ascolteranno idee diverse, più il nostro prodotto migliorerà veramente l'assistenza sanitaria di ogni utente. Quindi ti diamo il benvenuto e ti invitiamo a candidarti per Doctolib, indipendentemente dal tuo genere, religione, età, orientamento sessuale, etnia o disabilità. Unisciti a noi per costruire un mondo più in salute!*  

  

*Per garantire pari opportunità, ti invitiamo a escludere informazioni personali (ad esempio foto, età) dalle tue candidature. Se hai bisogno di adattamenti particolari per quanto riguarda il processo di selezione, ti preghiamo di informarci quanto prima.*  

  

*Tutte le informazioni fornite vengono trattate da Doctolib per la gestione delle candidature. Per i dettagli sul trattamento dei dati, clicca**qui**. Ti preghiamo di contattare* ***hr.dataprivacy(AT)doctolib.com*** *per domande o per esercitare i tuoi diritti.*

title: People and Culture Administrator - Categorie Protette L.68/99
description:
As a **People and Culture Administrator** you will support the People and Culture team by assisting with day\-to\-day administrative tasks. The role is a **part time \- entry level** job and will be based in Zuma restaurant in Rome


**Zuma** is a contemporary Japanese restaurant collection, inspired by the dynamic Izakaya dining style. Located in the iconic Palazzo Fendi, with a stunning restaurant and beautiful roof terrace overlooking the “eternal city”, this restaurant is an essential experience in Rome. Zuma Rome is proud to be part of the Azumi Collection.


**Main Duties**  

Your key responsibilities will include:

* Maintain and update employee records and HR files
* Handle attendance records, leaves, and basic HR administration
* Assist the wider People team with day\-to\-day administration
* Prepare reports and internal communications as required
* Manage relationships with schools, universities, and interns, including internship programs and coordination
* Support the People team in implementing People practices that drive performance, engagement and wellbeing
* Respond to internal and external people\-related enquiries and provide assistance where needed or redirect queries and correspondence to the appropriate team members
* Assist with note taking during team meetings and cross\-department catch ups
* Build strong relationships with our restaurant teams through open and positive communication and regular hands\-on, operational experiences


**What We Look For**  

Our ideal candidate embodies our values and brings:

* Previous internship or work experience in HR, administration, or office support is a plus
* Fluent English (spoken and written) is required
* Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail
* Ability to handle sensitive information with discretion
* Professional attitude and eagerness to learn
* Proficiency in Canva, MS Office (Word, Excel, Outlook)


**Benefits**  

We offer incredible career growth, international opportunities, and the chance to contribute to a world\-renowned brand, where every detail from the finest ingredients to our unrivalled atmosphere is crafted to perfection.
  

  

As part of our team, you’ll enjoy:

* World\-Class training, designed to inspire and educate
* Global opportunities, experience hospitality around the globe with our five incredible brands
* Family Meals are shared daily
* Rewards \& Recognition, we value our team and celebrate your contributions to our success with meaningful rewards and recognition
* Dining discount, enjoy exclusive discounts across the Azumi group, worldwide!


**Our Commitment to Inclusivity**  

We are an equal opportunities employer and welcome all applicants. If you require any assistance to make the recruitment process more accessible, please let one of our Talent Acquisition team know  

  

The annual gross salary for this position is €15\.000


* **You need to be registered to the Categorie Protette L.68/99**

title: Talent Acquisition Lead
description:
**About Eneba**

  

At Eneba, we're building an open, safe and sustainable marketplace for the gamers of today and tomorrow. Our marketplace supports close to 20m\+ active users (and growing fast!), provides a level of trust, safety and market accessibility unparalleled to none. We're proud of what we've accomplished in such a short time and look forward to sharing this journey with you. Join us as we continue to scale, diversify our portfolio, and grow with the evolving community of gamers.



We're the People Team. Besides finding and welcoming new team members, we ensure both individuals and teams are set up for success, play to their strengths, and are high\-performing. In doing so, we show care and empower our colleagues as they collectively grow Eneba.



We also care deeply about productivity and scalability. Day to day, you'll find us building and maintaining programs for talent acquisition, culture and inclusion, performance, learning and development, compensation, employee wellness and more.


### **Responsibilities**

* Take full ownership of a recruiter pod — keep your team focused, unblocked, and performing while carrying a substantial req load yourself.
* Provide clear direction to direct reports, translating team goals set by the Head of TA into individual priorities and expectations.
* Focus on people management will be of 15% of your time, the other 85% on delivery
* Run regular 1:1s focused on performance, pipeline health, and professional growth.
* Quality\-check your team's work across the full recruiting process — sourcing, outreach, pipeline management, interview experience, and candidate communication.
* Coach direct reports on recruiting craft, stakeholder management, and career development.
* Lead by example as a practitioner: take ownership of the more complex and senior searches within your pod's scope.
* Escalate people or performance concerns to the Head of TA in a timely way.

### **Requirements**


* 10\+ years of experience in recruitment, with at least 2 years in a team lead or pod leadership capacity.
* Demonstrated ability to carry a full req load while supporting the performance and development of others.
* Strong recruiting craft across the full hiring lifecycle, including:
* Sourcing and outreach strategy;
* Pipeline management and candidate experience;
* Stakeholder management and cross\-functional collaboration;
* Coaching and quality\-checking peers or junior team members.
* Clear communicator who sets expectations well and surfaces issues early.
* Keen sense of prioritisation — able to balance IC work with people responsibilities without dropping the ball on either.
* Empathetic team player who leads through example, not authority.

\#LI\-DNP

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Senior Director Brand and Content - 100% Remote - Europe
description:
**NOTE: This is a FULLY remote role, but the candidate must be within** **Europe** **to collaborate with their team, peers, and internal customers. You do not have to be in the specific country or city shown in this listing, but** **please only apply if you are physically based within** **Europe****.**


Trusted by 20,000\+ property managers worldwide, Hostaway is an industry leading, AI\-powered vacation rental management platform designed for professional short\-term rental operators. Hostaway brings everything together for greater efficiency and faster growth.

  

Profitable, high\-growth, and the first short\-term rental PMS unicorn . Join us at our most exciting stage yet! Learn more about our recent valuation and story here.


Hostaway is seeking a highly motivated Senior Director of Content \& Brand to join our team and drive our growth in the market.

### **About the role**


Hostaway is looking for a Senior Director of Brand and Content to build and lead the brand, content, and creative engine for our next stage of growth as a leading B2B SaaS platform for professional short\-term rental operators. This is a high\-impact SaaS marketing leadership role for someone who can shape our market narrative, elevate our category presence, and create content and creative programs that support awareness, pipeline, customer acquisition, and revenue growth.


You’ll build the function largely from the ground up, including the team, operating model, workflows, AI\-enabled production systems, and quality standards. This role will partner closely with Product Marketing, Demand Generation, Sales, Marketing Operations, and Product to ensure Hostaway’s brand, content, and creative output is aligned with SaaS GTM priorities, buyer journeys, product launches, demand generation, and measurable business outcomes.

### **Your mission**

* Build and lead a team that will own the modern content engine that produces high\-quality, high\-velocity editorial, thought leadership, and demand\-supporting content.
* Build and lead a small team that will define and own Hostaway's brand strategy, positioning, narrative architecture, and visual identity system for a fast\-growing global vertical SaaS company
* Build the in\-house Creative team from scratch including designers, copywriters, motion/video, and production talent.
* Across Content/Brand/Creative, utilize AI teammates who will drive efficiency while humans drive quality, designing workflows where generative tools handle research, drafting, variation, localization, and asset production, while humans own strategy, narrative, taste, and final quality.
* Partner closely with Product Marketing, Demand Generation, Sales, Marketing Operations and Product to align brand, content, and creative to support GTM priorities and drive revenue growth.
* Hire, mentor, and develop the brand, content, and creative team, making smart early bets on senior leaders, hands\-on makers, and hybrid AI\-native talent.
* Decide the right mix of in\-house team, agencies, freelancers, and AI\-augmented partners; manage vendor relationships and budget accordingly.
* Define KPIs across brand health, content performance, creative effectiveness, AI productivity gains, LLM/AI discoverability, and revenue contribution.
* 12\+ years of progressive experience across content, brand, and/or creative leadership, with significant experience in B2B SaaS marketing.
* 5\+ years of experience building and leading teams, including hiring senior talent and developing high\-performing creative or editorial professionals.
* Experience marketing to SMB or mid\-market SaaS buyers, ideally in a high\-velocity, transactional sales and marketing environment.
* Proven ability to build a SaaS marketing function or sub\-function from the ground up, including strategy, team structure, workflows, tools, and execution standards.
* Strong understanding of SaaS GTM motions, buyer journeys, product marketing, demand generation, lifecycle content, and sales enablement.
* Strong editorial instincts, storytelling ability, and creative judgment, with a clear sense of what makes B2B SaaS content and creative effective.
* Hands\-on fluency with AI tools for content, brand, and creative work, including a practical point of view on where AI improves quality, speed, and scale.
* Strong cross\-functional leadership skills, executive presence, and a track record of connecting brand, content, and creative work to measurable SaaS business outcomes such as pipeline, revenue, retention, and category authority.

### **What we offer**

* **Competitive Compensation:** We offer competitive pay based on market rates in the country of the applicant.
* **100% Remote:** Enjoy the freedom to work from anywhere within your country of residence—be it a co\-working space, your home office, or even your dining room table. The choice is yours. Just don’t ask to work in our office (we don’t have one).
* **Equity:** Every role in our company comes with valuable stock options in a fast\-growing and profitable company. This ensures we all share in the company’s success.
* **Values\-Driven Leadership:** Our Core Values are not just words we’ve written to make us feel good. We leverage them daily when making strategic and tactical decisions.
* **Professional Growth:** Our rapid growth offers unparalleled learning and development opportunities, along with a multitude of career advancement opportunities.
* **Annual Paid Leave:** The specific amounts vary by country and are aligned with country and/or contract\-specific norms.
* **Geographic Specific Benefits:** As an international employer, we offer different country\-specific benefits such as Health Insurance and Pensions in countries where these perks are customary. The specifics depend on the country of the applicant.
* **Dynamic Team Culture:** As a global company with team members in over 40 countries, our diverse and international culture fuels our innovation and creativity, providing a key pillar to our success (and making it a lot of fun to work here).


Thank you for your interest. If you apply for this role, you will receive an email from our Talent Acquisition team after your application has been reviewed alongside the qualifications for this role and the qualifications of others who have applied.

title: Configuration Manager
description:
Enginium
  
  

Tech. Team. Together.
  
  

Dal 2016 lavoriamo per dare forma a una realtà solida e in continua evoluzione, capace di ampliare il proprio impatto e guidare l’innovazione tecnologica. Siamo una società di consulenza ingegneristica e tecnologica che opera con efficacia, trasparenza e responsabilità, contribuendo ogni giorno alla crescita dei progetti e delle persone con cui collaboriamo. Siamo l’anima tech di Gi Group Holding, la prima multinazionale italiana del lavoro, e ogni giorno supportiamo le organizzazioni nel progettare, realizzare e governare la trasformazione digitale end‑to‑end. Lo facciamo integrando competenze specialistiche in R\&D Engineering, Business Applications, Cloud \& Infrastructure, Cyber Security \& AI, affiancando i nostri clienti dall’analisi dei processi fino alla messa in produzione di soluzioni scalabili, sicure e data‑driven.
  
  

In questi primi dieci anni abbiamo lavorato su progettualità ad alto contenuto tecnologico grazie alle competenze delle nostre Persone, cuore pulsante dell’azienda e motore che ci permette, ogni giorno, di generare valore.
  
  

Perché Enginium?
  
  

* Sfida Tecnologica: crediamo nel potere della tecnologia di trasformare il futuro e cerchiamo persone pronte a costruire questa evoluzione insieme a noi. Con un portfolio di aziende clienti diversificato e progetti ad alto contenuto innovativo, avrai l’opportunità di lavorare su soluzioni all’avanguardia e di crescere in contesti tecnici stimolanti.


La nostra Technical Direction ti accompagnerà nel tuo percorso, supportandoti nello sviluppo delle competenze e nell’esplorazione di nuove tecnologie. Entrerai in un ambiente che valorizza il talento, favorisce la crescita continua e che ti permetterà di mettere in gioco le tue passioni professionali.
  
  

* Formazione e crescita: Investiamo con convinzione nella crescita dei nostri talenti e nello sviluppo di nuove competenze. Attraverso percorsi strutturati di Career Path, accompagniamo le nostre persone in un’evoluzione continua delle competenze tecniche e trasversali, favorendo una crescita professionale coerente e sostenibile. Mettiamo inoltre a disposizione la nostra piattaforma Docebo, che offre contenuti dedicati sia allo sviluppo delle soft skill sia a tematiche tecniche avanzate, come sviluppo software, calibrazione motore, validazione software, product \& process engineering e system engineering.
* Presenza e supporto: garantiamo follow‑up costanti con il dipartimento HR e con l’area Business, assicurando presenza, ascolto e un supporto concreto nelle attività di people management lungo tutto il percorso professionale.
* I nostri vantaggi: Mettiamo al centro il benessere delle nostre persone attraverso un programma di Welfare aziendale dedicato ai dipendenti del Gruppo e l’accesso alla piattaforma Wellhub, pensata per favorire equilibrio e qualità della vita.


Nell’ottica di un potenziamento interno, ricerchiamo una figura di:
  
  

**Configuration Manager:**  

Quale sarà il tuo contributo in Enginium?
  
  

Il candidato/ la candidata, inserito/a all'interno di un team strutturato, nel settore Aerospazio e Difesa si occuperà di:
  
gestire e configurare sistemi complessi;
  
gestire le evoluzioni di prodotto;
  
gestire forniture esterne per aspetti configurativi.
  
  

**Chi stiamo cercando:**  

Laurea in Ingegneria Elettronica/Informatica/delle TLC/Biomedicale o similari;
  
  

Esperienza di almeno 6 mesi di tesi o tirocinio nella gestione di sistemi complessi;
  
Esperienza nella scrittura di requisiti tecnici;
  
Conoscenza del ciclo di vita di un prodotto;
  
Gradita conoscenza di normative internazionali in ambito difesa;
  
  

Conoscenza della lingua inglese, B1/B2\.
  
  

**Sede di Lavoro:** Roma
  
  

Il nostro Iter di Selezione
  
  

Dopo aver ricevuto la tua candidatura, il nostro team di Talent Acquisition valuterà con attenzione il tuo profilo. Se risulterà in linea con le opportunità aperte in Enginium, verrai ricontattato per un primo confronto telefonico, durante il quale approfondiremo il tuo percorso di studi, l’evoluzione professionale, le tecnologie maggiormente utilizzate, le tue motivazioni e gli obiettivi di crescita, sia in termini di settore sia di attività.
  
  

Il secondo step prevede un colloquio con l’area Business, in cui il tuo profilo verrà approfondito da un punto di vista tecnico. In questa fase entreremo nel dettaglio delle tue competenze e ti presenteremo le nostre attività e i progetti potenzialmente in linea con il tuo background, così da effettuare una valutazione a 360° e verificare il match tra ciò che desideri fare e ciò che possiamo offrirti in termini di ruolo e attività progettuali.
  
  

L’ordine e il numero degli step possono variare in funzione delle esigenze progettuali, mantenendo sempre un processo chiaro, trasparente e rispettoso dei tempi dei candidati e delle candidate.
  
  

In linea con il nostro impegno per un processo equo e trasparente, non richiediamo informazioni sulla tua retribuzione attuale in nessuna fase della selezione.
  
  

Sistema di Total Reward
  
**Range RAL (28\.000\-38\.000\):** Il range retributivo è flessibile e sarà determinato sulla base di parametri misurabili: seniority/esperienza, competenze, complessità del ruolo e livello di responsabilità, nel rispetto dei principi di equità interna e trasparenza.
  
**CCNL applicato:** CCNL Commercio e Terziario
  
Ticket/Welfare/Benefit
  
**Diaria e trasferte:** Per alcuni progetti, in relazione alla distanza tra la sede di assunzione e il luogo di svolgimento dell’attività presso il cliente, può essere prevista una diaria o un’indennità di trasferta, riconosciuta secondo criteri oggettivi e nel rispetto della normativa vigente e delle policy interne.
  
Piattaforma Formazione Docebo
  
Percorsi di Career Path dedicati
  
  

Pari opportunità e tutela dei dati
  
  

Crediamo nel valore della diversità e in un ambiente di lavoro inclusivo. Il nostro processo di selezione si svolge nel pieno rispetto dei principi di pari opportunità, garantendo un trattamento equo e privo di qualsiasi forma di discriminazione, diretta o indiretta, legata a genere, età, origine etnica, orientamento sessuale, convinzioni personali, condizioni individuali o altri aspetti non rilevanti ai fini professionali, in conformità alla normativa vigente e al nostro Codice Etico.
  
  

I candidati di qualsiasi genere (D.lgs n. 198/2006\) sono invitati a leggere l’informativa privacy ai sensi degli artt. 13 e 14 del Reg. EU 679/2016 al seguente indirizzo: https://www.enginium.eu/entra\-nel\-team/privacy\-candidati/.

title: Sales Associate- Castel Romano, Roma-PT24hr
description:
**Michael Kors is always interested in hearing from talented, globally\-minded individuals with a passion for fashion.**

**If you enjoy working in a creative, fast\-paced environment, then we would love to hear from you!**

—


**Who You Are:**

You bring enthusiasm for fashion and thrive in a dynamic retail environment. As a Michael Kors Ambassador, you represent the brand’s values with professionalism and integrity. You’re committed to creating inclusive, welcoming experiences for all customers, using strong communication, teamwork, and a proactive mindset to navigate challenges with energy and optimism.  



**What You’ll Do:**

· Lead by example as a MK Ambassador, ensuring exceptional customer experiences to drive loyalty and repeat business.


· Main tain client communication using all available clientelling apps and tools.


· Maintain floor and stock presence.


· Build and maintain effective communication with the management team to drive store performance.


· Use of Product Knowledge packs to become a Selling Expert to advise our customers with passion and confidence .

title: Italy Campaign Manager (Consultant)
description:
Role: Italy Campaign Manager (Consultant)


✒️ Status: Consultancy contract of 12 months, extendable


 4 working days a week


 Location: Rome, Italy


 Candidates need to have the right to work in Italy or be able to obtain a partita IVA


 Deadline for applications: 31/07/2026, at midnight

**Help build the next phase of Clean Cities in Italy**


We’re looking for a creative, politically astute and digitally confident campaigner\-organizer to help drive our next phase of growth in Italy. This is a role for someone who wants to build, test, mobilize, connect and win.


You might already have a few years of experience in campaigning, organizing, advocacy, communications or movement\-building. You might have worked on climate, transport, cities, energy or another public\-interest issue. You might be a digital\-native organizer, a creative campaigner with strong political instincts, or a policy\-minded communicator who knows how to turn complex issues into compelling public messages.


What matters most is that you can spot an opportunity, bring people together around an idea, and help turn momentum into action. In 2026, Clean Cities in Italy is entering a new chapter. We want to expand our presence beyond Milan, run sharper and more visible campaigns, build stronger local alliances, and activate more supporters online and ofﬂine. This role will be central to making that happen.

**The role**


You’ll help shape and deliver Clean Cities’ strategy in Italy, working closely with the Head of Italy Campaign, colleagues across Europe, and partners on the ground. You’ll lead or support campaigns, help grow our network in new cities, strengthen our supporter engagement and digital organizing, and contribute to public\-facing communications and political advocacy.


Some days this might mean planning a campaign intervention, writing sharp messaging or coordinating allies. Other days it might mean speaking with policymakers, helping organize supporters, spotting a media opportunity, or ﬁguring out how to turn a complex policy debate into something people care about. This role is for someone who enjoys both strategy and action, and who is excited by the challenge of helping build something.


This is a one\-year consultancy from September 2026 to September 2027\. Renewal is a strong possibility, subject to funding and performance.

**What you’ll do**

* Lead one or more campaigns as part of Clean Cities’ 2026 strategy in Italy, helping reﬁne the strategies, coordinating partners and allies, and connecting our campaigns to the wider movement for safer and more sustainable mobility.
* Depending on agreements with external partners, oversee the continuation of our *Mind the Gap!* and Road Code national campaigns.
* Support the expansion of our Milan\-based *Cargo Revolution* and *Dirty Delivering* campaigns into other Italian cities.
* Help grow Clean Cities’ presence beyond Milan, especially in cities such as Rome, Turin, Bologna, Florence, Genoa, Naples and Bari.
* Use key political moments — including local elections and urban transport policy debates — to build alliances and create openings for campaigning.
* Help strengthen our digital campaigning and organizing infrastructure in Italy, so we can better grow, activate and engage supporters.
* Lead on online and ofﬂine mobilization, communications and supporter engagement efforts.
* Work with colleagues, consultants, partners and allies across Italy and Europe to deliver ambitious, coordinated campaigns.
* Use a mix of tools and tactics — creative, political, digital and grassroots — to build public support and pressure for change.
* Engage conﬁdently with elected ofﬁcials, policymakers and their staff.
* Represent Clean Cities at meetings, events and conferences. Regular travel within Italy, and occasional travel to Brussels or elsewhere in Europe.
* Produce blogs, speeches, campaign copy, articles and social media content in collaboration with communications colleagues.


After a year in the role, success might look like:

* One or more visible, high\-impact campaigns delivered in one or more priority cities;
* Stronger partnerships and a more active network in Italy;
* A clearer foothold for Clean Cities in other cities beyond Milan;
* Better supporter activation and stronger digital organizing capacity;
* Sharper public messaging and greater political traction on our issues.

**Your Profile**


We’re looking for someone who is politically curious, collaborative, self\-starting and excited to make things happen.


You do not need to tick every box. If this role feels like a strong ﬁt, we’d encourage you to apply.


Must have:

* Experience in campaigning, organizing, advocacy, communications, coalition\-building or movement\-building.
* A strong interest in climate, transport, cities, energy, air quality, public space or related issues.
* The ability to bring people together around a shared goal and turn ideas into action.
* Strong communication skills, including the ability to make complex issues clear, engaging and politically relevant.
* Good judgement, initiative and the ability to manage your own work.
* Conﬁdence working across different audiences — from activists and allies to journalists and decision\-makers.
* Strong digital instincts and familiarity with the tools, platforms and methods used in campaigning, mobilization and communications.
* Native\-level of Italian and strong English skills.
* A base in Rome and willingness to travel regularly in Italy and occasionally to other European cities.


Nice to have:

* Experience in transport, climate, urban, energy or environmental campaigning.
* Experience working with local groups, grassroots networks or coalitions.
* Experience in digital mobilization, supporter journeys, CRM, email campaigning or social campaigning.
* Experience engaging with political institutions, local government or public administrations.
* Experience creating public\-facing content or helping shape campaign narratives.
* Familiarity with Italian urban mobility debates and local political dynamics.

**What we offer**

* A chance to play a central role in shaping the next phase of Clean Cities in Italy.
* Real responsibility and room to grow.
* Work that connects campaigning, organizing, policy, politics and communications.
* A ﬂexible \[0\.8 FTE] setup with remote working arrangements.
* A collaborative, ambitious and mission\-driven team.
* Travel expenses covered and IT equipment as needed.
* Co\-working space as needed in our two ofﬁces in Rome.

**How to apply**


You can apply directly via our website. The deadline is **July 31, 2026, at midnight.** Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis. Shortlisted candidates might be asked to complete a short task during the recruitment process.

**About us**


The Clean Cities Campaign is a European coalition of more than 150 grassroots and civil society organizations working for healthier, safer and more liveable cities. We campaign for active, shared and electric mobility, and for a rapid shift to


zero\-emission urban transport.


Clean Cities is part of Transport \& Environment, Europe’s leading clean transport NGO. In Italy, we’ve built a strong reputation as a collaborative and credible voice on sustainable urban mobility. We work with local governments, campaign partners and civil society allies to push for cleaner transport, more public space for people, and a faster transition away from polluting vehicles. We’re on a mission to decarbonize and democratize transport in Italian cities.

**Equal opportunities**


We want to build a team that reﬂects the diversity of the cities and communities we work with. We strongly encourage applications from people who are underrepresented in the environmental, transport and mobility sectors. Even if you don’t meet every requirement listed above, we’d still like to hear from you.

* **️ IMPORTANT SECURITY NOTICE FOR APPLICANTS**

**Protecting Your Personal Data against Recruitment Scams:** T\&E takes digital data privacy and information security seriously. Please note that all legitimate communication from our talent acquisition team will exclusively originate from an official corporate domain ending in **@**transportenvironment.org (or cleancitiescampaign.org) or directly via secure automated channels on our managed applicant tracking platform.

* **No Unverified Communication:** T\&E does not authorize external recruitment agencies or headhunters to contact candidates using unverified, generic webmail accounts such as @gmail.com, @yahoo.com, or @outlook.com.
* **No Charging or Fees:** T\&E will never request sensitive financial information, banking credentials, passport copies, or any form of payment at any stage of our recruitment process.
* **Verification Protocol:** All legitimate T\&E vacancies are listed on our 2 websites https://jobs.transportenvironment.org/ **or** https://cleancitiescampaign.org/vacancies/

If you receive a suspicious solicitation and wish to verify its legitimacy, please cross\-reference the vacancy on our 2 websites or forward the communication directly to the HR team at hrsupport@transportenvironment.org before sharing any personal documentation.

Realtà specializzata nei servizi di posa in opera di chiusure edili, schermature solari e coperture modulari per esterni seleziona profili operativi da integrare stabilmente nelle proprie squadre di cantiere.


Siamo alla ricerca di una figura coscienziosa, dinamica e motivata a inserirsi in un contesto professionale solido.

La nostra proposta e inquadramento:

Sede di Svolgimento dell'Attività:

title: Lifecycle Specialist, Employee Relations and Transitions - EMEA
description:
**About Remote**
----------------


Remote is solving modern organizations' biggest challenge – navigating global employment compliantly with ease. We make it possible for businesses of all sizes to recruit, pay, and manage international teams. With our core values at heart and future focused work culture, our team works tirelessly on ambitious problems, asynchronously, around the world. You can find Remoters working from 6 different continents (Antarctica left to go!) and all of our positions are fully remote.  

  

With Innovation as one of the core values, we have built Automation and AI capabilities into the requirements for every role.  

  

We encourage every member of the Remote team to bring their talents, experiences and culture to the table to help us build the best\-in\-class HR platform.

If you are energetic, curious, motivated and ambitious, be part of our world. Apply now and define the future of work!**This is not an active job opening, but a way for us to connect with talent as we plan for future opportunities.** If you prefer to interview only for currently open roles, we kindly ask that you wait until an active position aligns with your career goals.



We're always looking for exceptional talent to join us in our mission to make work accessible for everyone, everywhere. While this specific role isn't currently open, we're planning for strategic growth in 2026 and beyond. This posting is part of our future opening pipeline initiative, which helps us connect with outstanding professionals early on.



By expressing your interest in this role, you'll have the opportunity to start a conversation with us about your expertise and learn more about Remote's vision and goals. It's also a chance to explore how your career goals might fit into Remote's exciting journey of global growth. We'll keep your information on file for 1 year and be in touch if we see a match in that time.


**What this job can offer you**
-------------------------------



As a critical extension of our clients' HR teams, we navigate and advise on the intricate paths of global employment with unmatched speed, expertise, and precision. The vision of the Lifecycle team at Remote is not just about maintaining the gold standard in HR practices; it's about elevating it, integrating cutting\-edge technology solutions, and enriching customer experiences in over 80\+ countries. On this team, your work directly influences our ability to sustain and extend our compliance coverage, continuously enhance our customer journeys, and significantly increase our operational capacity. You're not just part of a team; you're at the forefront of shaping the future of work, ensuring every interaction is fast, intuitive, and profoundly impactful. Dive into a role where your passion for innovation, commitment to excellence, and drive to make a global difference aligns with our mission to empower organizations worldwide to employ anyone, anywhere — compliantly.



The Lifecycle Specialist, Employee Relations \& Transitions manages employee exits for Remote's external employees with a focus on legal compliance and providing delightful customer service. Additionally, the specialist provides first\-level Employee Relations support, guiding clients through workplace concerns and coordinating appropriate resources when needed.



As part of a larger employee Lifecycle team, the Specialist is also expected to contribute to process improvements and initiatives in line with Remote's strategic goals and values.


**Key Responsibilities**
------------------------


### **Offboarding**


* Manage and resolve end\-to\-end employee exits across multiple international jurisdictions, prioritizing a superior offboarding experience for Remote's clients and their employees.
* Provide sound guidance and manage clients' requests on various HR matters, including but not limited to termination, resignation and transfer procedures, employment contracts, and redundancy processes, ensuring legal compliance and best practices.
* Lead and facilitate meetings with clients and external employees, including but not limited to workforce reduction consultation meetings, administrative hearings, negotiations in separations and settlements, providing guidance on terminations, and delivering termination outcomes.
* Influence and negotiate positive outcomes in complex terminations, balancing multiple stakeholders with competing interests.
* Liaise with and coordinate various internal and external stakeholders to ensure compliance in offboarding activities and audits, including but not limited to payroll, legal counsel, government bodies, and trade unions.
* Draft, tailor and administer all termination and supplementary documents with accuracy and attention to detail, ensuring employee records are maintained with strict adherence to privacy and confidentiality regulations.
* Process offboarding on Remote's internal HRIS platform, collaborating with various internal stakeholders, such as Customer Success, Legal, Payroll, Benefits, Time and Attendance, amongst other verticals, to ensure all aspects of the employee exit are administered correctly and promptly.
* Be the subject\-matter expert and respond to internal and external queries on all offboarding matters in conjunction with Lifecycle's internal stakeholders.
* Navigate clients through Remote's Employee Relations resources and guides, helping them understand applicable policies and processes.
* Conduct preliminary assessment of workplace concerns, identifying key facts and applicable local regulations.
* Coordinate with internal and external stakeholders to ensure appropriate handling of employee relations matters.
* Support clients in understanding progressive disciplinary approaches and documentation requirements.
* Monitor ongoing employee relations cases, ensuring timely follow\-up and coordination with relevant stakeholders.
* Actively identify opportunities for and contribute to process improvements, automation, product development and overall customer and employee experience through the offboarding and employee relations processes.

**Key Requirements and Charactristics**
---------------------------------------


* Experience as a HR Advisor or HR Business Partner, with generalist HR competencies and exposure across the employee lifecycle.
* Proven experience with a strong background in managing voluntary and involuntary exits (terminations) with care, sensitivity and legal compliance; and providing comprehensive HR support and guidance on employment laws and regulations, spanning various jurisdictions.
* Ability to interpret and apply industrial instruments, analyze complex HR issues, undertake research, provide practical solutions and effectively communicate recommendations to clients and internal stakeholders when operating in ambiguous and unfamiliar environments.
* Basic understanding of employee relations principles, progressive discipline approaches, and workplace investigation fundamentals.
* Excellent communication, influencing, conflict management, and negotiation skills, with the ability to confidently lead difficult conversations to a positive outcome, often where parties have conflicting interests, maintaining professionalism and strict confidentiality.
* Business\-level (advanced) proficiency in written and spoken English, with additional languages considered a plus.
* Aptitude and appetite to innovate and optimize processes, continuously identifying and implementing opportunities for automation and championing best practices.
* Efficiency in operations, with an awareness of the importance of thorough record\-keeping and data integrity.
* Tech\-savvy, with the ability to successfully collaborate asynchronously on various tools, and adapt to new and evolving systems.
* Ability to work autonomously and cohesively in an international team in a fast\-paced, asynchronous, remote environment with multiple priorities. Willingness to work flexible hours as needed.
* Proactive and self\-motivated with a strong sense of ownership and accountability.
* **Demonstrates strong automation and AI capabilities and AI fluency.**

**Practicals**
--------------


* **You'll report to:** Manager Employee Lifecycle, Employee Relations \& Transitions
* **Team:** Lifecycle, Employee Relations \& Transitions
* **Location**: EMEA

**Application process**
-----------------------


* Interview with recruiter


* Take Home Exercise


* Interview with future manager


* Interview with team members (no managers present)


* Interview with Senior Manager


* Bar Raiser


* Prior employment verification check
**Benefits**
------------


Our full benefits \& perks are explained in our handbook at remote.com/r/benefits. As a global company, each country works differently, but some benefits/perks are for all Remoters:
* work from anywhere
* flexible paid time off
* flexible working hours (we are async)
* 16 weeks paid parental leave
* mental health support services
* stock options
* learning budget
* home office budget \& IT equipment
* budget for local in\-person social events or co\-working spaces
**How you'll plan your day (and life)**
---------------------------------------



We work async at Remote which means you can plan your schedule around your life (and not around meetings). Read more at remote.com/async.



You will be empowered to take ownership and be proactive. When in doubt you will default to action instead of waiting. Your **life\-work balance** is important and you will be encouraged to put yourself and your family first, and fit work around your needs.



If that sounds like something you want, apply now!


**How to apply**
----------------


* Please fill out the form below and upload your CV with a PDF format.


* **We kindly ask you to submit your application and CV in English, as this is the standardised language we use here at Remote.**


* If you don't have an up to date CV but you are still interested in talking to us, please feel free to add a copy of your LinkedIn profile instead.



Not only do we encourage folks from all ethnic groups, genders, sexuality, age, abilities, disability status and any other under\-represented group to apply, but we prioritize a sense of belonging. We have 4 ERGs (Women, Disability, Queer, Minorities in Tech) who meet regularly with the People team. During your interviews and beyond, we ask \& encourage anybody who needs an accommodation to request one from their recruiter.

  


We will ask you to voluntarily tell us your pronouns at interview stage, and you will have the option to answer our anonymous demographic questionnaire when you apply below. As an equal employment opportunity employer it's important to us that our workforce reflects people of all backgrounds, identities, and experiences and this data will help us to stay accountable. We thank you for providing this data, if you chose to.



At Remote, we embrace AI as a valuable tool while prioritizing human creativity and authenticity. We look forward to meeting candidates who balance innovation with genuine expertise and experience. To learn more about Remote's AI guidelines check see here.  

  

*Please note we accept applications on an ongoing basis.*

title: Head of People & Labour Relations Italy (9-month Fixed Term Contract)
description:
By clicking the “Apply” button, I understand that my employment application process with Takeda will commence and that the information I provide in my application will be processed in line with Takeda’s Privacy Notice and Terms of Use. I further attest that all information I submit in my employment application is true to the best of my knowledge.

**Job Description**
-------------------


Previa verifica dei requisiti professionali, costituisce titolo preferenziale l’appartenenza alle Categorie Protette ai sensi dell’art 1 L. 68/99 e/o alle categorie di lavoratori che risultino percettori di interventi a sostegno del reddito e/o risultino disoccupati a seguito di procedure di licenziamento collettivo e/o lavoratori delle aziende del territorio di riferimento coinvolte da tavoli di crisi attivi presso il MIMIT.


The **Head of People \& Labour Relations Italy** is an exciting 9 month fixed term contract working within the People \& Labour Relations Function. This function is enterprise focused, dedicated to supporting our Employee Relations and Labour Relations agenda across multiple geographies. In addition, it will have specific focus on all business units within Italy across the key organization’s capabilities of business transformation, Labour Relations, Employee Relations, HR Governance \& HR Policy, and Policy Framework.


 The role will be a member of the People \& Labour Relations community and will help shape the LR/ER agenda. The role will comprise of the following core areas of responsibilities:

**ER/LR** \- the **Head of People \& Labour Relations Italy** will take the lead on the provision of Labour Relations and Employee Relations advice and guidance within the two geographies, specifically in relation to escalated cases where the risks are potentially significant. The role will be responsible for providing consultative support and management of complex employee \& labour relations cases to employees and managers, in partnership with key stakeholders, and will be key to creating a consistent employee experience across the different BUs in Italy.

**Manage Strategic Projects related to the People \& Labour Relations Strategy \-** This position is responsible for the management and delivery of Employee Relations (ER) and Labour Relations (LR) projects across all business units, functions, and employees within Italy. This role will also be instrumental in supporting, enabling, and delivering deployment of global systems and operational changes aligned with the Data and Digital strategic imperative at Takeda. Working on global to local initiatives the person in this role will ensure timely project management whilst advising as a subject matter expert in this field to ensure that the change is delivered moothly and communicated effectively in line with Takeda values and leadership behaviours.

**Policy Development to enable Employee Experience \-** Create Consistent HR Policy Framework and Country HR Policy Suite for Italy. Aligned with global / regional HR guidance, take a lead in developing and defining policies that position Takeda competitively in the country. Support policy implementation and socialize policies at the appropriate level ensuring the correct governance and procedures are in place in line with Global processes and frameworks.

**Country Governance** \- Create \& lead in Country HR governance Process for Italy. Create platform to align HR issues that affect HR Strategy in country or multiple LR/ER impacts across different business units. Facilitate through the appropriate cadence and operating rhythm alignment and decision making for in country HR and consultation with Business Leaders on Country Wide HR Topics.

**Organisational Capability** – Enhance the organisational capability within Italy through partnership with HR and Business Units.

***ACCOUNTABILITIES***

**Employee Relations**

* In line with global processes and frameworks, roll out and implement agreed Employee\-Related Investigations Policies \& Process.
* Provide counsel to HRBL and HRP on complex employee relations issues, proposing fair, consistent, and timely resolution while ensuring legal compliance and evaluating risk mitigation to the Company
* Lead creation and delivery of local processes, tools and training related to employee relations including on matters of labour negotiation (written answers prior to negotiation, info on wage negotiation) and coaching management and HR partners
* Investigate complex (escalated) cases, particularly involving senior managers or where the risks are potentially significant, acting as the central point of contact for Ethics \& Compliance and Legal on those matters.
* Create a collaborative dispute resolution environment by managing workplace mediation process and facilitate advanced problem solving where possible to resolve disputes
* Roll out specific frameworks and lead ER case management such as Performance improvement counseling, Employee investigations, Involuntary termination documentation, Disciplinary and RIF documentation across the region as needed and in line with global initiatives
* Strengthen Takeda’s people strategy to mitigate investigations and case management by driving and harmonizing people initiatives and annual processes and establish a broad and understandable compass for employees.
* Maintain up to date knowledge of Global ER \& LR trends and regulations, country labour law and tribunal cases
* Ensure that the ER dashboard is up to date with relevant action plans
* Work with the owner of other HR communication (E.G. Reward COEI) on their communication content, planning and approaches and collaborate with a range of stakeholders to ensure that HR\-related messages impacting all BU and Functions in region are appropriately cascaded

**Labour Relations**

* Communicate the Global Labour Relations Strategy regionally and locally, as required, in accordance with the Labour Relations Framework sharing relevant information with the Strategy Enablement Group.
* Roll out of Local Education Materials for HR and Business Leaders on Labour Relations
* Work closely with the Labour Relations Strategy \& EWC Lead to ensure positive labour relations is maintained across the region. Where required attend and influence the make\-up of External Enterprise Agreements and ensure HRPs are aware of consultation requirements and act accordingly
* Work closely with the other People \& Labour Relations colleagues to ensure positive labourrelations are maintained across the designated geography. The People \& Labour Relations willensure that local policies and procedures that cover ER matters are in place, sufficient,communicated and act accordingly.

**Project Management \& Governance**

* Provide end to end project management on regional and global initiatives (such as HR policy introduction and roll out) as required and alongside the Project Lead.
* Support the deployment of global systems across the region by providing technical advice and guidance to project leads in the design phase of projects from an ER/LR perspective
* Provide end to end technical advice, expertise and support in HR change proposals and initiatives ensuring that correct governance is in place and implemented effectively, providing progress reports as required
* Innovate, socialise, and deliver simplified, innovative solutions with stakeholders that enhance the employee experience across the region, as required
* Aligned with global / regional HR guidance, take a lead in developing and define policies that position Takeda competitively on matters such as maternity, family care, leave, Rehire eligibility, Programs/counseling due to life event changes, work schedule, over time, Local employment policies, reports \& action plan (e.g., disability, affirmative action), Workplace health \& safety policy, local severance plan development.
* Where required support Labour relations projects, initiatives and activities including the management of acquisition integration/ divestment communication/consultation etc.
* Create in line with Global Guidance the appropriate regional and country governance for HR.

**Critical Skills and Competencies**

* A competent specialist in Labour or Employee Relations
* Ability to manage the complexity of implementing business change
* Demonstrates an ability to deal with ambiguity, balance and mitigate risks and provide credible advice
* Strong collaboration skills with all relevant stakeholders in HR and the business
* Experience in working in a unionized environment and/or with Works Councils an advantage
* Proven track record in implementing initiatives to drive exceptional employee experience
* Ability to influence cross multiple stakeholder groups and coach leaders
* Excellent verbal and written communication skills required to convey complex information in a clear and concise manner

**Experience and Education**

* Bachelor’s degree or equivalent ideally with background in labour or employment law
* At least 5 years’ experience in Labour and/or Employee Relations with evidence of LR/ER thought\-leadership
* Significant experience in complex and multi\-country HR organization with proven understanding of local and regional diversity of thought and application
* Accountability in previous roles for multiple geographies is necessary
* Sound understanding of employment regulations and labour law across multiple geographies
* Human Resources experience in the health care or pharmaceutical industry preferred.
* Proficient in multiple languages
* Strong partnership and collaboration focus to create sustainable processes and increase efficiency
* HR Project management experience is desirable but not essential


At Takeda, we are committed to fostering a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workplace where all employees feel valued, respected, and empowered. We believe that our differences drive innovation and excellence, and we strive to create an environment where everyone can thrive. Ti occuperai di migliorare e velocizzare il nostro processo di selezione e di aumentare la qualità delle persone inserite in azienda.

**Ottimizzazione**

* Analisi delle performance delle selezioni
* Miglioramento continuo del processo
* Proposta di nuove soluzioni e strumenti

**CHI CERCHIAMO**

**Requisiti fondamentali:**

* Esperienza concreta nel recruiting e selezione del personale

Senior Solution Consultant
description:
*To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.*

Job Category


Customer Success
Job Details


**About Salesforce**

Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all.


Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce.


**DEPARTMENT DESCRIPTION**  

  

Salesforce Professional Services focuses on delivering strategic engagements that define transformational opportunities and articulate clear plans for execution of change programs. We rely on our team’s expertise and specialisms to engage with executive\-level customers to agree on specific business goals and actions to ignite the full value of Salesforce. Our approach considers people, process, and technology — and a successful engagement includes recommendations to the business for change to support deeper, more relevant use of the Salesforce platform.
  

Salesforce Technical Consultants deliver solutions that transform our customers’ businesses and their customers’ experiences. This role involves working with teams to discover, design, code and deploy Salesforce Platform solutions.
  

  

**OVERVIEW OF THE ROLE**  

  

Salesforce is currently seeking a Senior Technical Consultant to join our Professional Services team within the Customer Success Group (CSG). Senior Technical Consultants work as part of a team demonstrating technical thought leadership of customer and partner engagements. These lead to the successful delivery of solutions, including enterprise integrations using middleware tools and platforms. Through our extended onboarding program of formal certification, internal training, knowledge transfer, hands on experience and closely working with other Consultants, Architects and Project Managers you will become a Salesforce Domain Authority at the forefront of our offerings, delivering outstanding customer success.
  

  

Senior Technical Consultants are technically hands on with our products, and are responsible for evaluating and implementing technical solutions for customers.
  

The ideal Senior Technical Consultant is a demonstrated thought leader in technical aspects of customer and partner engagements that lead to the successful delivery of enterprise desktop, cloud, and/or mobile solutions, including enterprise integrations leveraging middleware tools and platforms. They also identify and proactively handle risk areas and contribute to seeing issues through to project completion.
  

  

**YOUR IMPACT**  

  

Through our extended onboarding program of formal certification, internal training, knowledge transfer, hands on experience and closely working with other Consultants, Architects and Project Managers you will become a Salesforce Domain Authority at the forefront of our offerings, delivering outstanding customer success.


* Become a highly certified and recognized Salesforce expert.
* Help clients consistently meet/exceed goals and metrics.
* Contribute to customer success through front and backend solution development and release management.
* Deliver high client satisfaction (CSAT).
* Provide guidance on the application and integration development best practices, Enterprise Architecture standards, functional and technical solution architecture \& design, environment management, testing, and Salesforce Platform education.
* Drive the creation of application and technical design documents which leverage Salesforce best practices and effectively integrate Salesforce into the customer’s infrastructure.
* Troubleshoot key customer implementation issues and demonstrate the ability to drive to a successful resolution. Lead the evaluation of business and technical requirements from a senior level.
* Identification and proactive management of risk areas and commitment to seeing an issue through to complete resolution.
* Manage customer expectations; negotiate solutions to complex problems with both the customer and third\-party partners.
* Build relationships with business partners, internal and external, and contributing to broader goals and growth beyond the scope of a single customer engagement.

  

  

**REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS**

* 5 years of experience minimum in IT space
* Hands\-on technical and solution implementation experience in a relevant software domain, as well as coding experience in object oriented languages.
* Ability to analyze, design, and optimize business processes via technology and integration.
* Ability to support customers and project members in rationalising and deploying emerging technology.
* Ability to work independently and as part of a distributed team.
* Ability to learn new technologies as well as good presentation, communication and facilitation skills.
* Available to travel regionally and occasionally internationally depending on customer needs.
* Degree or equivalent relevant experience required. Experience will be evaluated based on the core competencies for the role (e.g. extracurricular leadership roles, military experience, volunteer roles, work experience, etc.)
* Proficiency in both English AND Italian languages
* Strong background in object\-oriented programming (e.g., Java) and understanding of REST/SOAP API design, microservices, and event\-driven architectures.

Unleash Your Potential


When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world.


Accommodations

description:
*To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.*

Job Category

Job Details


**About Salesforce**

Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all.


Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce.


**DEPARTMENT DESCRIPTION**  

  

Salesforce Professional Services focuses on delivering strategic engagements that define transformational opportunities and articulate clear plans for execution of change programs. We rely on our team’s expertise and specialisms to engage with executive\-level customers to agree on specific business goals and actions to ignite the full value of Salesforce. Our approach considers people, process, and technology — and a successful engagement includes recommendations to the business for change to support deeper, more relevant use of the Salesforce platform.
  

Salesforce Technical Consultants deliver solutions that transform our customers’ businesses and their customers’ experiences. This role involves working with teams to discover, design, code and deploy Salesforce Platform solutions.
  

  

**OVERVIEW OF THE ROLE**  

  

Salesforce is currently seeking a Technical Consultant to join our Professional Services team within the Customer Success Group (CSG). Technical Consultants work as part of a team demonstrating technical thought leadership of customer and partner engagements. These lead to the successful delivery of solutions, including enterprise integrations using middleware tools and platforms. Through our extended onboarding program of formal certification, internal training, knowledge transfer, hands on experience and closely working with other Consultants, Architects and Project Managers you will become a Salesforce Domain Authority at the forefront of our offerings, delivering outstanding customer success.
  

  

Technical Consultants are technically hands on with our products, and are responsible for evaluating and implementing technical solutions for customers.
  

The ideal Senior Technical Consultant is a demonstrated thought leader in technical aspects of customer and partner engagements that lead to the successful delivery of enterprise desktop, cloud, and/or mobile solutions, including enterprise integrations leveraging middleware tools and platforms. They also identify and proactively handle risk areas and contribute to seeing issues through to project completion.
  

  

**YOUR IMPACT**  

  

Through our extended onboarding program of formal certification, internal training, knowledge transfer, hands on experience and closely working with other Consultants, Architects and Project Managers you will become a Salesforce Domain Authority at the forefront of our offerings, delivering outstanding customer success.


* Become a highly certified and recognized Salesforce expert.
* Help clients consistently meet/exceed goals and metrics.
* Contribute to customer success through front and backend solution development and release management.
* Deliver high client satisfaction (CSAT).
* Provide guidance on the application and integration development best practices, Enterprise Architecture standards, functional and technical solution architecture \& design, environment management, testing, and Salesforce Platform education.
* Drive the creation of application and technical design documents which leverage Salesforce best practices and effectively integrate Salesforce into the customer’s infrastructure.
* Troubleshoot key customer implementation issues and demonstrate the ability to drive to a successful resolution. Lead the evaluation of business and technical requirements from a senior level.
* Identification and proactive management of risk areas and commitment to seeing an issue through to complete resolution.
* Manage customer expectations; negotiate solutions to complex problems with both the customer and third\-party partners.
* Build relationships with business partners, internal and external, and contributing to broader goals and growth beyond the scope of a single customer engagement.

  

  

**REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS**

* 5 years of experience minimum in IT space
* Hands\-on technical and solution implementation experience in a relevant software domain, as well as coding experience in object oriented languages.
* Ability to analyze, design, and optimize business processes via technology and integration.
* Ability to support customers and project members in rationalising and deploying emerging technology.
* Ability to work independently and as part of a distributed team.
* Ability to learn new technologies as well as good presentation, communication and facilitation skills.
* Available to travel regionally and occasionally internationally depending on customer needs.
* Degree or equivalent relevant experience required. Experience will be evaluated based on the core competencies for the role (e.g. extracurricular leadership roles, military experience, volunteer roles, work experience, etc.)
* Proficiency in both English AND Italian languages
* Strong background in object\-oriented programming (e.g., Java) and understanding of REST/SOAP API design, microservices, and event\-driven architectures.

  

  

**PREFERRED**

* Salesforce Certifications
* Experience with the Salesforce Platform and / or other CRM products.
* Experience with Salesforce Revenue Cloud and / or other products in the billing and revenue space (Conga, CloudSense, Zuora).
* Ability to build and manage relationships both internally and externally.
* Experience working in a senior consulting or technical lead role working with matrix teams at a top\-tier consultancy or system integrator.
* Familiarity with AI\-assisted development tools

Unleash Your Potential


When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. title: Land Acquisition Manager (f/m/d)
description:
**Workload: 100%**


Are you passionate about renewable energy and eager to make a tangible impact? Join us as a Land Acquisition Manager and help secure the foundation for future photovoltaic (PV) projects. With us, you’ll work in a dynamic environment that values collaboration, innovation, and sustainability.


**What you will do:**


* Identify and engage with landowners of targeted lands.
* Visit identified sites to assess their potential.
* Analyze opportunities for land acquisition and project feasibility.
* Use specialized software to estimate technical parameters of PV plants.
* Prepare technical and financial offers for land acquisition projects.
* Draft and negotiate land rights contracts with landowners.
* Monitor regulatory frameworks and ensure compliance.
* Collaborate proactively to update GIS systems in line with regulatory changes.
* Maintain CRM systems with scheduled updates and reporting.
**What you bring \& who you are:**


* Experience in land acquisition, ideally in the renewable energy sector.
* Strong negotiation skills and ability to build trust with stakeholders.
* Analytical mindset with proficiency in software tools for PV plant estimation.
* Knowledge of regulatory frameworks related to land rights and energy projects.
* Team\-oriented and proactive approach to collaboration.
* Ideally, experience with GIS systems and CRM tools.
Even if you don’t meet all the requirements, we encourage you to apply. We value diverse perspectives and are excited to hear from you!


**About the team:**


You’ll be part of a motivated and supportive team that shares a common goal: advancing renewable energy solutions. We foster an open and inclusive culture where every voice matters and innovation thrives.

title: Tecnico Audioprotesista Rieti (RI)
description:
**Job Description****Il ruolo**
  

  

Siamo sempre orgogliosi di accogliere nuovi audioprotesiste/i nei nostri negozi per aiutarci a far riscoprire alle persone tutte le emozioni dei suoni. Per noi è importante che i clienti si sentano ascoltati e compresi \- siamo davvero a disposizione per migliorare la loro vita.


Per rafforzare la nostra leadership nell'area, abbiamo un'opportunità per un/a audioprotesista che si voglia unire al nostro team di Rieti (RI), Lazio.

**Di cosa ti occuperai?**

* Valuterai le esigenze uditive dei tuoi clienti attraverso interviste mirate e test audiologici, applicando i protocolli Amplifon;

* Identificherai e offrirai la soluzione migliore in base alle difficoltà uditive del cliente, assicurando il miglior adattamento grazie alle tue competenze audiologiche;

* Gestirai l'intera trattativa commerciale con i clienti al fine di proporre la miglior soluzione a seconda delle specifiche esigenze;

* Supporterai il cliente durante l'intero percorso di Amplifon, dal test, alla prova e all'applicazione, fino al follow\-up post\-vendita e alla cura della riparazione e della manutenzione degli apparecchi acustici, alimentando un rapporto duraturo;

**Qualifiche e competenze:**

* Laurea triennale in Tecniche Audioprotesiche (L/SNT03\) o titolo equipollente.

* Ottime capacità organizzative e relazionali.

* Doti di negoziazione commerciale.

**Cosa cerchiamo?**

* Orientamento al cliente, empatia e capacità di creare solide relazioni con i clienti grazie a buone doti di comunicazione e alla capacità di farli sentire a proprio agio e ascoltati.
* Orientamento al business, con buone capacità di negoziazione e una mentalità proattiva per raggiungere gli obiettivi.

* Spirito di squadra, capacità di lavorare in team e favorire le relazioni con i colleghi, la classe medica e tutti gli interlocutori.

**Cosa significa lavorare in Amplifon?**

* Un ambiente stimolante e dinamico con opportunità di costruire una carriera su misura.

* Opportunità di formazione di alto livello per sviluppare continuamente le tue competenze e conoscenze professionali.

**Retribuzione e condizioni**

* Contratto applicato: CCNL Commercio

* Pacchetto retributivo: inserimento al IV livello CCNL Commercio (RAL da 26\.000 a 28\.000 € in base ad esperienza e competenze), a cui si aggiungerà un piano provvigionale connesso agli obiettivi di business tempo per tempo condivisi

* Orario: Full time


Sono al contempo attive ricerche di collaborazioni per lavoratori autonomi con formula di incarico a Partita Iva. In questi casi è previsto un compenso variabile determinato in forma di provvigione ed eventuali ulteriori benefit concordati in fase di trattativa correlati a obiettivi specifici.


Amplifon è un'azienda che garantisce pari opportunità e si impegna a offrire un ambiente di lavoro diversificato ed equo. Crediamo che valorizzando la nostra unicità e rispettando le nostre differenze possiamo raggiungere risultati migliori, e che la diversità arricchisca la nostra cultura. Incoraggiamo le candidature di ogni genere, provenienza geografica e background individuale.


Valutiamo le candidature esclusivamente sulla base delle competenze, dell'esperienza e del potenziale, senza discriminazioni legate a genere, età, origine, nazionalità, disabilità, orientamento sessuale, identità di genere, religione, stato civile, situazione personale o qualsiasi altra caratteristica tutelata dalla legge.


In linea con questo impegno, e nel rispetto degli obblighi previsti dal D.Lgs. 96/2026 attuativo della Direttiva UE 2023/970 e dalla relativa normativa nazionale di recepimento, nonché delle procedure interne dedicate, Amplifon non richiede né raccoglie informazioni sulla retribuzione precedente o sulla qualifica professionale dei candidati: tali informazioni non vengono richieste durante i colloqui, via e\-mail né tramite agenzie di selezione, headhunter o qualsiasi altra terza parte coinvolta nel processo.


Se hai bisogno di assistenza durante il processo di candidatura o di selezione a causa di una disabilità, ti invitiamo a comunicarcelo. Vogliamo garantire che il nostro processo sia accessibile a tutti.


Al termine del processo di selezione, l'Azienda potrà proporre un pacchetto retributivo diverso da quello inizialmente indicato, sulla base dell'esperienza, delle competenze e del profilo complessivo del candidato. Eventuali differenze saranno definite nel rispetto dei requisiti di trasparenza e sulla base di criteri oggettivi e non discriminatori.


La presente ricerca è condotta da Amplifon Italia per conto di una società partner, garantendo un processo trasparente e professionale nel rispetto integrale del dlgs. 96/2026\. Il/La candidato/a selezionato/a sarà inserito/a direttamente dal nostro partner, con cui avrà modo di entrare in contatto nel corso del processo di selezione.
